HANOI (Reuters) – Vietnam will start to offer COVID-19 vaccine booster shots from December, with medical staff, teachers and people over 50 prioritized, the health ministry said on Monday.
The ministry said Vietnam will offer booster shots from December 10, six months after the date of full vaccination, according to a report in state media.
Vietnam has already vaccinated 55 million people with at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine and 36 million people with two doses.
The country expects to receive 155 million doses of COVID-19 vaccines this year
